incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion leading to exfiltration of customer data, which attackers began selling on a cybercrime forum.
incidents.impact: Vercel confirmed the breach, notified a small number of affected customers, and urged them to rotate keys and secrets. The ShinyHunters group claimed responsibility.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack forced the city to shut down its IT network; surrounding Leon County disconnected its network as a precaution — typical pattern of a ransomware response.
incidents.impact: Several city websites and public services remained unavailable days after the attack. No group has publicly claimed responsibility.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a vulnerability in the platform's rsETH bridge to withdraw funds via a LayerZero-based attack path.
incidents.impact: Approximately $292 million in crypto assets stolen, making it the largest crypto heist of 2026 to date.

incidents.vector: Attackers pre-staged fake token pools and then exploited vulnerabilities in the protocol's margin trading feature to drain assets.
incidents.impact: Hackers stole $18.4 million in crypto assets from the Rhea Finance DeFi platform.

incidents.vector: Data exfiltrated from McGraw Hill's Salesforce tenant; part of the broader Salesforce-targeted theft wave from 2025.
incidents.impact: Personal data of 13.5 million users — including real names, home addresses, emails, and phone numbers — was leaked after McGraw Hill refused to pay a ransom to the ShinyHunters group.

incidents.vector: Sophisticated distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack that intensified over the course of a day, with follow-on attacks throughout the week.
incidents.impact: BlueSky experienced a prolonged intermittent outage for almost a full day starting late April 15. Follow-on DDoS attacks failed to bring the site down again.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and NATO military/government bodies
incidents.vector: Long-running credential phishing and adversary-in-the-middle campaign against Microsoft 365 email accounts, with stolen data accidentally exposed on an attacker-controlled server.
incidents.impact: At least 284 inboxes compromised between Sep 2024 and Mar 2026, including Ukraine's Specialized Anti-Corruption Prosecutor's Office, ARMA, and the Prosecutor's Training Center; plus 67 Romanian Air Force accounts, 27 Hellenic National Defense General Staff inboxes, Bulgarian officials in Plovdiv, and Serbian military/academic figures.

incidents.vector: Hackers breached an internal network storing customer data; the exact intrusion vector has not been disclosed.
incidents.impact: South Africa's largest bank disclosed a customer-data breach — the third South African bank hack this year.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion; specifics of initial access not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Ransomware attack disrupted Autovista's services across Europe and Australia.

incidents.vector: Data stolen from Hallmark's Salesforce tenant as part of a multi-victim Salesforce targeting wave in 2025.
incidents.impact: Personal data of 1.7 million Hallmark customers leaked by the ShinyHunters group after Hallmark refused to pay a ransom.

incidents.vector: Maliciously crafted PDF files exploited CVE-2026-34621 (CVSS 8.6), an arbitrary code execution flaw in Adobe Acrobat and Reader triggered by merely opening a document.
incidents.impact: A highly obfuscated PDF silently fingerprinted victims and exfiltrated system information and local files to a C2 server, positioning for follow-on RCE/sandbox-escape payloads. Evidence shows exploitation in the wild since at least Nov 2025; Adobe patched on April 11, 2026.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion claimed by the Qilin group.
incidents.impact: Die Linke shut down its IT network after the attack. The party says employee records were stolen but no member data was compromised.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack on healthcare software supplier ChipSoft, whose HiX electronic health record system is used by roughly 70% of Dutch hospitals; the company advised customers to cut secure VPN connections after systems were compromised.
incidents.impact: ChipSoft disabled Zorgportaal, HiX Mobile, and the Zorgplatform while rebuilding systems. At least 11 Dutch hospitals disconnected ChipSoft software; LUMC paused a new EHR rollout. Z-CERT said disruption was logistical only and no critical care processes stopped. Possible unauthorized access to patient data has not been ruled out.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into NYK's marine fuel (bunker) procurement system at the end of March; specifics not disclosed.
incidents.impact: NYK suspended use of the platform and said some employee data was accessed and likely stolen. NYK is part of Ocean Network Express, the world's sixth-largest container shipping company.

incidents.victim: US and allied government, military, and critical infrastructure networks
incidents.vector: GRU Unit 26165 exploited known vulnerabilities in TP-Link and MikroTik SOHO routers to reset DNS settings to attacker-controlled resolvers, then performed adversary-in-the-middle attacks against Microsoft Outlook Web Access to harvest OAuth tokens, credentials, and emails.
incidents.impact: More than 18,000 routers compromised across 120+ countries, affecting 200+ organizations and 5,000 consumer devices — including ministries of foreign affairs, law enforcement, Afghan government, and an unnamed European national identity platform. FBI's 'Operation Masquerade' neutralized the US portion under court authorization; campaign communications have ceased.

incidents.victim: US water, wastewater, energy, and local government networks
incidents.vector: Iran-affiliated actors targeted internet-exposed Rockwell Automation / Allen-Bradley PLCs and SCADA/HMI displays (including CVE-2021-22681), manipulating project files and control-screen data; also reportedly expanding to Siemens devices and FactoryTalk software.
incidents.impact: Joint FBI/Pentagon/NSA/CISA/EPA/DoE advisory warns of 'operational disruption and financial loss' at US critical infrastructure organizations since at least March 2026, with a water treatment plant in Minot, ND and an Indiana county government among confirmed incidents. Unit 42 telemetry identified roughly 5,600 exposed Rockwell/Allen-Bradley SCADA devices globally.

incidents.victim: Northern Ireland Education Authority (C2K schools network)
incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the centralized C2K IT system that serves the vast majority of Northern Ireland schools; nature of intrusion not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Access to teaching materials, assignments, exam revision, and communications disrupted for roughly 300,000 pupils and 20,000 teachers. The Education Authority shut down the system and worked with Capita and an incident response firm to rebuild access, prioritizing pupils sitting exams. No confirmed data exfiltration at time of reporting.

incidents.vector: CVE-2026-35616 — an improper access control vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClient EMS (CVSS 9.1) — exploited in the wild since late March, one week after disclosure.
incidents.impact: CISA and Singapore's CSA issued urgent advisories; CISA added the bug to its KEV Catalog and required federal agencies to apply the hotfix by April 9. FortiClient EMS is widely used across governments. This is the second FortiClient EMS zero-day disclosed in three weeks.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, financial, and professional services organizations (AU/UK/US)
incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware affiliates (Storm-1175) exploit vulnerable internet-facing assets — including CVE-2026-23760 (SmarterMail) and CVE-2025-10035 (GoAnywhere MFT) — often one week before public disclosure, then use ConnectWise ScreenConnect, AnyDesk, and SimpleHelp to move from initial access to ransomware deployment in as little as 24 hours.
incidents.impact: Microsoft's analysis highlights Medusa's ability to exploit vulnerabilities before widespread patching. Recent Medusa victims include the University of Mississippi Medical Center (fully reopened March 2) and Passaic County, NJ.

incidents.vector: Phishing granted unauthorized access to a limited set of files; the Silent Ransom Group posted client materials on March 30 and shared screenshots of negotiation attempts.
incidents.impact: Unauthorized access to dated files for 10 clients of the 2,400-lawyer firm, which has represented major corporations and a US president's campaigns. Affected clients were notified. Hackers claimed to target the firm's Federal Circuit litigation team.

incidents.vector: Suspected DPRK actor compromised a package within Axios — a JavaScript library with ~100 million weekly downloads — and slipped malicious code into a release, executing cross-platform (macOS, Windows, Linux) payloads.
incidents.impact: Before removal after ~3 hours, the compromised package was downloaded an estimated 500,000+ times. Elastic detected the compromise within minutes of publication via AI-driven registry monitoring. OpenAI had to issue Mac app updates as a direct downstream consequence.

incidents.vector: DPRK operators built a fake trading company and met Drift employees at conferences, spending months building trust before compromising devices via a malicious Apple TestFlight app and poisoned VS Code extensions, then multi-signing treasury transactions.
incidents.impact: Attackers stole $280 million from Drift's treasury. Investigators linked the activity to DPRK cluster Citrine Sleet (UNC4736 / AppleJeus).
